circular.title: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Climate Finance and Management of Climate Change Risks) Directions, 2025
circular.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued directions for commercial banks to manage climate-related financial risks. The goal is to encourage the flow of credit to sustainable activities while preventing greenwashing. Banks must implement Board-approved policies and financing frameworks, ensuring that proceeds from green deposits are allocated to specific eligible categories.

clauses.text: These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Climate Finance and Management of Climate Change Risks) Directions, 2025.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
Applicable to Commercial Banks, including SBI and corresponding new banks, but excluding SFBs, Payment Banks, and Local Area Banks.
A bank desirous of raising green deposits shall put in place comprehensive Board-approved policies on Green Deposits and Financing Framework.
The Board shall be responsible for reviewing the bank’s green deposit initiatives through a review report.
clauses.summary: Obligation to review green deposit initiatives

clauses.text: The Board-approved policy shall lay down all aspects of issuance and allocation. A copy must be available on the bank's website.
clauses.summary: Obligation to have a policy on green deposits

clauses.text: Green deposits shall only be denominated in Indian Rupees.
clauses.summary: Obligation to denominate green deposits in INR

clauses.text: Banks are not permitted to offer differential rates of interest on green deposits. Interest must be paid as per agreed terms irrespective of the allocation/utilisation of proceeds.
clauses.summary: Obligation to not offer differential interest rates

clauses.text: The Board-approved Financing Framework (FF) must cover eligible green activities, project evaluation/selection processes, allocation reporting, and temporary allocation particulars.
clauses.summary: Obligation to have a Financing Framework

clauses.text: A bank shall arrange to carry out an external review of their FF.
clauses.summary: Obligation to have an external review of the FF

clauses.text: Before implementation, a copy of the FF and the external reviewer's opinion must be on the bank's website.
clauses.summary: Obligation to publish the FF and external review

clauses.text: Proceeds must be allocated based on the official Indian green taxonomy (or the interim list provided in the directions).
clauses.summary: Obligation to allocate proceeds to eligible green activities

clauses.text: Fund allocation shall be subject to an annual independent third-party verification/assurance.
clauses.summary: Obligation to have annual third-party verification

clauses.text: A bank shall assess annually the impact of green finance activities through an Impact Assessment Report with the assistance of external firms.
clauses.summary: Obligation to have an annual Impact Assessment Report

clauses.text: Banks shall place Third-Party Verification and Impact Assessment Reports on their websites.
clauses.summary: Obligation to publish reports on the website

clauses.text: A review report must be placed before the Board within three months of the end of the financial year, covering amounts raised, lists of projects, and verification/impact findings.
clauses.summary: Obligation to submit a review report to the Board

clauses.text: Annual financial statement disclosures must follow the RBI (Commercial Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
clauses.summary: Obligation to follow financial statement disclosure directions

obligations.text: Implement Board-approved policies on Green Deposits and a Financing Framework.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must have Board-approved policies for green deposits and financing framework.

obligations.text: Denominate green deposits exclusively in Indian Rupees (INR).
obligations.plain_english: Banks must only offer green deposits in Indian Rupees.

obligations.text: Ensure green deposit interest rates and tenors comply with general deposit rules without offering differential rates.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must not offer different interest rates for green deposits.

obligations.text: Conduct an external review of the Financing Framework (FF) and publish it on the bank's website.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must have an external review of their financing framework and publish it on their website.

obligations.text: Allocate proceeds only to eligible green activities and exclude prohibited sectors.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must only use green deposit proceeds for eligible green activities.

obligations.text: Park unallocated green proceeds temporarily only in Level 1 High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) for a maximum of one year.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must temporarily park unallocated green deposit proceeds in high-quality liquid assets.

obligations.text: Undertake annual independent third-party verification of fund allocation.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must have an annual independent verification of their green deposit fund allocation.

obligations.text: Assess and publish an annual Impact Assessment Report for financed green projects.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must publish an annual report on the impact of their green finance activities.

obligations.text: Submit a comprehensive review report to the Board of Directors.
obligations.plain_english: Banks must submit a review report to their Board of Directors.

prohibitions.text: Banks are not permitted to offer differential rates of interest on green deposits.
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot offer different interest rates for green deposits.

prohibitions.text: Exclusions: Fossil fuel extraction, production, or distribution.
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for fossil fuel activities.
Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for nuclear power generation.
Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for direct waste incineration.

prohibitions.text: Exclusions: Alcohol, weapons, tobacco, gaming, or palm oil industries.
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for certain industries.

prohibitions.text: Exclusions: Biomass using feedstock from protected areas.
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for biomass from protected areas.
Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for landfill projects.

prohibitions.text: Exclusions: Hydropower plants larger than 25 MW.
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot use green deposit proceeds for large hydropower plants.

definitions.definition: Activities/projects meeting requirements prescribed in paragraph 14.
definitions.plain_english: Eligible green activities as defined by the RBI

definitions.definition: An interest-bearing deposit for a fixed period with proceeds earmarked for green finance.
definitions.plain_english: A deposit with proceeds used for green finance

definitions.definition: Lending to or investing in green activities/projects that contribute to climate risk mitigation, adaptation, resilience, biodiversity, or nature-based solutions.
The practice of marketing products/services as green when they do not meet the required criteria.
definitions.plain_english: Misleading marketing of non-green products as green

references.circular_number: RBI (Commercial Banks - Interest Rate on Deposits) Directions, 2025
